basics.name: Maya LeBlanc
basics.label: Engineering Student — Robotics
basics.summary: First-year Engineering student at McMaster University with research and design experience spanning robotics, computational biology, and artificial intelligence. My technical interests centre on robotics systems engineering — from mechanical design and CAD to perception, grasp planning, and robot operating frameworks — with a focus on applications in space, defence, and healthcare contexts where engineering complexity serves a clear purposeful mission.

work.name: McMaster Robotics Manipulation and Autonomy Lab (RMAL)
work.summary: Undergraduate researcher in the Robotics Manipulation and Autonomy Lab under Dr. Gary Bone (McMaster Mechanical Engineering). Working on grasp planning and manipulation research.
highlights: Developed power grasp detection algorithm for 3D objects via multi-height mesh analysis in MATLAB
Computational geometry, algorithm design, and robotics manipulation

work.name: McMaster Mars Rover Team (MMRT)
work.summary: Sole mechanical designer for the communications subsystem of a Mars-analog rover competing in the University Rover Challenge (URC).
highlights: Designed 2-DOF servo-actuated pan-tilt camera mount with custom 1:2 spur gear train and 608 ball bearing in SolidWorks
Designed dual GPS antenna mounts with 1-metre baseline separation for spatial diversity and improved signal reliability
All components 3D printed in PLA with FDM manufacturing

work.name: McMaster Faculty of Health Sciences — Lupus Clinic
work.summary: Invited to join Dr. Konstantinos Tselios's research team based on prior independent research conducted through Aletheia. Working on development of a novel diagnostic tool for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus.
highlights: Extension of ISEF 2024 4th Place Grand Award research in computational immunogenomics
Collaboration with physician-scientist Dr. Konstantinos Tselios

work.name: Aletheia Research Initiative
work.summary: Founded and lead an independent research initiative to pursue original scientific work outside of institutional boundaries.
highlights: Computational immunogenomics research resulting in ISEF 2024 4th Place Grand Award
Published in International Journal of High School Research (2025) and Canadian Science Fair Journal (2022)
McMaster Mars Rover Team — Communications System Mechanical Designer
Robotics Manipulation and Autonomy Lab (RMAL) — Undergraduate Researcher

publications.name: Analysis of CD16+/- Monocytes and CD4+ T Cells to Identify Novel Gene Signatures and Develop Diagnostic Tool for SLE
publications.publisher: International Journal of High School Research
publications.summary: First-authored research in computational immunogenomics. Acceptance rate ~20%, expert-reviewed by Dr. Bowdish, Dr. Tselios, and Dr. Breznik.

publications.name: Carbon Capture Depletion Using Catalyzed Photosynthesis to Promote Crop Growth
publications.summary: First-authored research on catalyzed photosynthesis for carbon capture. Acceptance rate ~8%, expert-reviewed by Nathan Badry.

projects.name: 2-DOF Pan-Tilt Camera System
projects.summary: Servo-actuated pan-tilt camera mount for URC Mars Rover. Custom 1:2 spur gear train, 608 ball bearing, SolidWorks, PLA FDM. 360° pan, 180° tilt.
highlights: SolidWorks
FDM 3D Printing
Servo actuation
Custom gear train

projects.name: Power Grasp Detection on 3D Objects via Multi-Height Mesh Analysis
projects.summary: Grasp planning algorithm for 3D objects using multi-height mesh analysis. MATLAB, computational geometry, algorithm design.
highlights: MATLAB
Computational geometry
Algorithm design

projects.name: Dual GPS Antenna Mounts
projects.summary: Primary and secondary GPS antenna mounts with 1-metre baseline separation for spatial diversity. URC Mars Rover.
highlights: SolidWorks

Antenna diversity
